description_long: This object is a partially intact, weathered airplane fuselage with significant structural damage and missing sections, exposing its interior. The fuselage features a row of windows and a door, with remnants of the cockpit area at one end and a broken tail section at the other. The wings are incomplete, with only fragments remaining attached to the main body, and the landing gear is either missing or not visible. The surface is predominantly light-colored with visible signage, including blue and orange panels, and the overall appearance suggests abandonment or a crash scenario. The structure is hollow, with open access points and visible internal supports. The airplane's materials appear to be primarily metal, with some plastic and glass components, and the surface shows signs of wear and exposure.
description: A damaged, hollow airplane fuselage with broken wings, visible signage, and exposed interior, showing signs of abandonment and weathering.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the airplane from an angle highlighting the broken cockpit area, partial wings, and exposed interior structure.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the side of the fuselage with visible signage, a staircase, and more detail of the fragmented wing and open door.
description_view_2: View 2 focuses on the rear of the airplane, showing the broken tail section and the hollow, open-ended fuselage.
description_view_3: View 3 displays the opposite side of the fuselage, emphasizing the signage, open structure, and remnants of the wing and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight, partially broken wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: No engines visible on the remaining structure.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer, though the tail section is broken and incomplete.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible or missing from the structure.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted cockpit, partially intact, with an enclosed canopy and visible window frames.
